result_text: CORRETTO da retro-grade L0-L3 + controllo positivo T1 (i vecchi verdetti "regge / repeat=0" erano artefatti del proxy): (a) a <=800 tok rotate32 E static K23 sono ENTRAMBI L0 funzionale -- il repeat=0 di rotate32 era un'illusione del proxy (loop CSS header/.grid/.card, corruzioni `#l Lime`/`##0f0`, mai `<body>`), static K23 L0 con repeat=1: stessa fine, diverso proxy. (b) Il grade L0 a <=800 sul cyberpunk NON e' attribuibile alla mask: budget-confound dimostrato da T1 (full no_pace L0 sul cyberpunk a 800 E 2000 tok, greedy e sampled, 13/13 repeat=0 e CSS sempre pulito e mai loop; pagina completa L2 solo a 3498 tok, finish=stop). (c) Cio' che RESTA attribuibile alla mask e' la FIRMA loop/corruzione: il full non loopa MAI (13/13 repeat=0), le mask K23/rotate32 loopano dentro `<style>`. (d) A 2000 tok emerge separazione: W100 rotate32 = L1 (apre con `<div>` ma niente bottone, struttura sporca), W50 rotate32 = L2 (body + card + `<button onclick>`, tronca prima dello `<script>`); a ctx8192 il W50 n=1 emise il primo `</html>` del corpus (0/89 prima), ma la replica M1a n=3 NON lo riproduce: 0/6 seed emettono `</html>` (W50 = L0,L0,L0; W100 = L0,L1,L0; median ~2.5-2.6 t/s locali) -> era un rollout fortunato, ctx8192 da solo non rende il documento funzionale su questa config, e "W50>W100" non e' un finding robusto (runs/ds4/20260710_m1a_w50_w100_ctx8192_n3/). Stopper client M1b n=3 = L2,L0,L1: intercetta parte dei collassi, nesso stop->pagina-funzionale non ancora dimostrato (runs/ds4/20260710_m1b_w50_stopguard_ctx8192_n3/). La metrica giusta a basso budget e' la firma di degenerazione, non il livello L da solo. t/s invariati (static 3.03-3.39 / rotate32 2.61-3.03 a c128/c256; costo rotate 0.36-0.45).

experiment: Local 3060 no-think launcher sanity after observe
quality_signal: Useful config fix, not a full performance fix. Hidden readback must stay off until GPU-side SPEX exists. `prefill_wait_wrap=0` improves visible TTFT. The remaining bottleneck is prefill selected-direct plus a resident cache that does not hit across these requests; increasing cache slots is not available on 12GB VRAM. Next work: selected-direct/resident reuse, or hidden-SPEX GPU scoring/prefetch; dynamic cold compression remains separate.
setup_text: UI launcher changed locally: `DS4_SPEX_HIDDEN_PREFETCH=0`, trace routing off by default, `DS4_PACE_PREFILL_APPLY=1`, `DS4_PACE_PREFILL_WAIT_WRAP=0`, cache slots 258. Test prompt: no-think `Rispondi solo OK`, max_completion_tokens=1, repeated twice after restart.
result_text: Hidden readback failure reproduced first: a 77-token prompt kept running after client abort, no first token for >124s, and finished only on shutdown at 258.252s with 87 tokens. With hidden-readback off and prefill wait on: 21.9s cold-ish, 9.66s warm. With wait off: 10.07s first run, 8.81s second run. Log: `prefill_apply tok=0 keep=64`, fattorino async 16.89 GiB in 1581 ms, prompt done 7.458s then 6.690s, decode 2.197s then 1.695s. Observe JSONL still shows selected_direct:resident 86:86, hit_rate 0, misses 516, direct_loads 3912, cap 258. VRAM was nearly full, about 11.8/12.3 GiB used.
verdict: Useful config fix, not a full performance fix. Hidden readback must stay off until GPU-side SPEX exists. `prefill_wait_wrap=0` improves visible TTFT. The remaining bottleneck is prefill selected-direct plus a resident cache that does not hit across these requests; increasing cache slots is not available on 12GB VRAM. Next work: selected-direct/resident reuse, or hidden-SPEX GPU scoring/prefetch; dynamic cold compression remains separate.

experiment: K0 first-token A/B: prefill mask versus warmup mask
quality_signal: Quality hypothesis supported but costly: B preserves K0 for the first 50 tokens and starts cleaner, but costs about +23s total on this short HTML sample. For coding/HTML, prefer B until a better split exists: learn/prefetch from prompt without applying mask before token 50. Needed runtime feature: separate prompt-derived prefetch from prompt-derived mask.
setup_text: Same no-think HTML prompt, 73 prompt tokens, 64 completion tokens, temp 0. A: `DS4_PACE_PREFILL_APPLY=1`, `PREFILL_WAIT_WRAP=0` means mask K64 before first generated token. B: `DS4_PACE_PREFILL_APPLY=0` means K0 for warmup; log confirms `PACE learned tok=50 keep=0`, then `PACE descent tok=51 keep=64`.

experiment: K0 warmup then K23: direct cut versus step-down
quality_signal: On this 3060 short HTML test, step-down does **not** pay: warmed direct K23 is about 19s faster than warmed stepped and has fewer prefetch churn points. This supports the user's prior local finding. Quality is still fragile at K23 for code/HTML, so the safe conclusion is not "K23 is quality-good"; it is "if we are going to cut to K23, gradual downward steps are slower than the direct cut here." Keep K0 for the first 50 tokens; use direct cut for speed tests; solve quality with better predictive/prefetch or dynamic tiering, not with coarse K steps.

experiment: Micro-breath after first cut: +1 expert ramps
quality_signal: Micro16 is the only promising variant: essentially no throughput penalty and it delays/avoids the hard breath. But it did **not** fix code quality on this sample, and micro8 was visibly too aggressive. Do not make prebreath default yet. If revisited, test micro16 on longer generations and with a higher trigger (`PREBREATH_DRIFT` around 0.05-0.08) so it starts when drift actually rises, not immediately after descent.
setup_text: Same no-think HTML prompt, 73 prompt tokens, 240 completion tokens, temp 0, `PREFILL_APPLY=0`, hidden SPEX off, observe tiering on. Baseline direct: `KEEP=23,PREBREATH=0`. Micro16: `KEEP=23,KEEP_STEP=1,PREBREATH=1,PREBREATH_DRIFT=0.03,PREBREATH_EVERY=16,PREBREATH_KEEP_MAX=64`. Micro8: same but `EVERY=8`.
result_text: Baseline direct 240: prompt 9.824s; K0 first 50 at 2.79 t/s; K23 at tok 51; hard `breath(ngram)` at tok 200 to K96; finish 84.816s, decode 74.991s; output had valid colors early but degraded syntax (`max-width 1200`). Micro16: prompt 10.473s; K23 at tok 51; prebreath K24 at tok 52, then K25/K26/.../K35 by tok 237; no hard breath; finish 84.742s, decode 74.269s; output avoided some syntax loss but invented CSS tokens (`#ciseRed/#ciseWhite`). Micro8: K24 at tok 52, then to K45 by tok 233; no hard breath; finish 85.722s, decode 75.728s; output worse lexically (`#cciano`, `#purple`, `70-bar`). All observe runs still selected-direct dominated with hit_rate 0.
verdict: Micro16 is the only promising variant: essentially no throughput penalty and it delays/avoids the hard breath. But it did **not** fix code quality on this sample, and micro8 was visibly too aggressive. Do not make prebreath default yet. If revisited, test micro16 on longer generations and with a higher trigger (`PREBREATH_DRIFT` around 0.05-0.08) so it starts when drift actually rises, not immediately after descent.

experiment: HTML160 ID trace: cache-cap pressure estimate
quality_signal: This quantifies the 3060 bottleneck: current effective cap ~258 is far below the useful compact working set. Dynamic compression/tiering should be framed as increasing **effective resident capacity** (toward 512-1024 expert slots equivalent) rather than just shaving bytes off rarely touched cold data. Cap 258 still leaves about two thirds of compact requests as misses in this trace.
setup_text: Same no-think HTML prompt as J13, 73 prompt tokens, 160 completion tokens, `KEEP=23`, `PREFILL_APPLY=0`, hidden SPEX off. Temporarily enabled `DS4_EXPERT_TIERING_LOG_IDS=1`; saved trace as `/root/ds4_tiering_observe_html160_ids.jsonl` (2.4 MB).

LRU sim over compact IDs: cap64 0.0000, cap128 0.0000, cap258 0.3396, cap512 0.5927, cap1024 0.7438. Worst layers by direct loads: L1 1158, L0 1145, L2 1148, L19 1073, L4 1069.
verdict: This quantifies the 3060 bottleneck: current effective cap ~258 is far below the useful compact working set. Dynamic compression/tiering should be framed as increasing **effective resident capacity** (toward 512-1024 expert slots equivalent) rather than just shaving bytes off rarely touched cold data. quality_signal: Important correction: do not reintroduce the old hidden-readback mistake. Reading 4096-float `ffn_norm` and scoring on CPU is bad; asynchronously handing off a tiny topK result is the likely safe bridge from GPU scoring to CPU-driven SSD prefetch.
setup_text: Source read of `/root/ds4` CUDA streaming cache: `ds4_gpu_stream_expert_cache_seed_experts_async` takes host `int32_t *expert_ids`, sorts priorities on CPU, computes model offsets, and drives CPU/SSD-backed copies.
result_text: Conclusion documented in `docs/SPEX_INTEGRATION_PLAN.md`: a literal zero-host consumer is not compatible with the current loader because SSD prefetch needs CPU-visible IDs. The right next design is async D2H of only the compact topK IDs (6-23 ints) into pinned host buffers, event-polled by the next-layer prefetch path; if not ready, skip and rely on miss fallback.
verdict: Important correction: do not reintroduce the old hidden-readback mistake. Reading 4096-float `ffn_norm` and scoring on CPU is bad; asynchronously handing off a tiny topK result is the likely safe bridge from GPU scoring to CPU-driven SSD prefetch.

quality_signal: This is the first positive shape for the user's idea: "all experts compressed, prompt router promotes likely hotset, decode dynamically promotes misses." On-demand-only is too stall-prone, but prompt-preload cuts runtime promotions enough to justify a runtime prototype. Still not a speed/quality claim: DS4 must wire a real cold sidecar/promote path, and cap1024 native hot capacity may exceed 12GB VRAM unless it lives as host-native staging or replaces existing selected-direct mechanics.
setup_text: Extended `scripts/analyze_tiering_observe.py` with prompt-derived preloading: `--tier-prefill-rows auto` uses the first layer cycle as prompt/router signal, ranks `selected` experts, preloads up to cap, then replays decode rows. Reused J31 run2 trace; no new DS4 generation.
result_text: Prompt-preload improved decode replay materially. With `warm_grace=64`, `cold_scale=0.33`, cap1024 reached hot-hit `0.8489`, served `0.8586`, runtime promotions `5836/41280 = 0.1414`, total promotions including preload 6860. With immediate demotion to compressed (`warm_grace=0`, all cold in RAM at x0.33), cap1024 still reached hot-hit `0.8489`, promotions `6237/41280 = 0.1511`, scaled footprint about 13.13 GiB. Cap512 was cheaper but weaker: hot-hit `0.6811`, promotion-rate `0.3189`, footprint about 10.54 GiB.
verdict: This is the first positive shape for the user's idea: "all experts compressed, prompt router promotes likely hotset, decode dynamically promotes misses." On-demand-only is too stall-prone, but prompt-preload cuts runtime promotions enough to justify a runtime prototype. Still not a speed/quality claim: DS4 must wire a real cold sidecar/promote path, and cap1024 native hot capacity may exceed 12GB VRAM unless it lives as host-native staging or replaces existing selected-direct mechanics.

experiment: DS4 runtime prompt-preload promotion observe policy
quality_signal: Runtime plumbing milestone. DS4 can now observe the exact policy shape online instead of only offline in `reap-loop`. This validates prompt->preload->decode state transitions, but it is not yet a speed win. Next step: exact native sidecar/promote path, then compressed cold RAM backing.
setup_text: Commit `/root/ds4` `e3167cc tiering: add prompt-preload promotion observe policy`; env `DS4_EXPERT_TIER_POLICY=observe_promote`, `DS4_EXPERT_TIER_PROMOTE_CAP`, optional `DS4_EXPERT_TIER_PROMOTE_VERBOSE`. This is metadata-only: no sidecar, no lossy compression, no changed expert tensors.
result_text: Build OK: `make ds4-server CUDA_ARCH=sm_86`. Smoke with prompt `Rispondi solo OK.`, max 2, cap1024, `LOG_IDS=1`: output `OK.`, JSONL rows 129 with phases `prompt:43`, `decode:86`; first decode row finalized the prompt hotset with `tier_policy_preloaded=1024`; decode totals in smoke were hot_hits 342, promotions 174, evictions 174.
verdict: Runtime plumbing milestone. DS4 can now observe the exact policy shape online instead of only offline in `reap-loop`. This validates prompt->preload->decode state transitions, but it is not yet a speed win. Next step: exact native sidecar/promote path, then compressed cold RAM backing.

experiment: DS4 lossless cold RAM sidecar path
quality_signal: Correctness/plumbing milestone, not a performance recipe. Lossless RAM proves exact byte addressing/checksum/fallback and frees us to replace the blob payload with a smaller cold format. It also proves why native lossless cannot be the final answer: even a tiny prefill can duplicate ~12 GiB and stall badly. Next target is a smaller cold RAM payload plus prompt-preloaded promotion, not SSD miss recovery.
setup_text: Commit `/root/ds4` `859d3db tiering: add lossless cold RAM sidecar path`; env `DS4_EXPERT_COLD_RAM_LOSSLESS=1`, optional `DS4_EXPERT_COLD_RAM_PREFILL=1`, `DS4_EXPERT_COLD_RAM_VERIFY=1`. The sidecar stores exact native quantized expert bytes in RAM blobs and copies H->D from there before falling back to GGUF/mmap. Default is off; prefill use is off unless explicitly enabled.
result_text: Build OK: `make ds4-server CUDA_ARCH=sm_86`. Decode-only smoke did not materialize because the 2-token run hit resident cache. Prefill-enabled correctness smoke (`prefill=1`, `verify=1`, prompt 15, gen 1) returned `OK`, zero failures, materialized 1836 entries, 2220 reuses, 4056 copies, 12.393 GiB RAM blobs. It was intentionally slow: prompt 158.896s, finish 166.687s. Server was restored afterward to default with cold sidecar off.
verdict: Correctness/plumbing milestone, not a performance recipe. Lossless RAM proves exact byte addressing/checksum/fallback and frees us to replace the blob payload with a smaller cold format. It also proves why native lossless cannot be the final answer: even a tiny prefill can duplicate ~12 GiB and stall badly. Next target is a smaller cold RAM payload plus prompt-preloaded promotion, not SSD miss recovery.

experiment: Real expert compressibility + CQ1 cold-format lab
quality_signal: Lossless cold RAM is a dead end for memory savings. The first runtime candidate should be hot native + cold CQ1, probably `cq1g32` on the 3060 to leave RAM margin. This is still a quality-risk prototype: weight nRMSE is high (~0.58), so it needs opt-in subset tests and graded outputs before any claim.
setup_text: Added `scripts/gguf_inspect_ds4.py` GGUF payload sampling and `scripts/ds4_cold_codec_lab.py` CQ1 error/RAM estimator. Tests: `pytest tests/test_gguf_inspect_ds4.py tests/test_ds4_cold_codec_lab.py -q` -> 5 passed.
result_text: Real `/root/models/ds4-2bit.gguf`: routed experts are `IQ2_XXS` gate/up + `Q2_K` down, 6.75 MiB/expert, ~72.56 GiB for all 11008 routed experts. Generic lossless compression is a negative: zlib1 avg ratio 0.9931, zlib6 0.9923, lzma0 0.9966, bz2 expands, entropy ~7.95 bits/byte. `ds4-staticQ4.gguf` has the same routed expert formats, so it is not a smaller expert source. CQ1 lab on 768 random blocks: dot nMAE about 0.029 avg across `IQ2_XXS/Q2_K`; estimates `cq1g256` 34.27 GiB all experts, `cq1g64` 40.31 GiB, `cq1g32` 48.38 GiB. Dynamic estimates: `1024` hot native + cold `all:cq1g32` ~= 50.62 GiB; `2048` hot native + cold `all:cq1g32` ~= 52.88 GiB.
verdict: Lossless cold RAM is a dead end for memory savings. The first runtime candidate should be hot native + cold CQ1, probably `cq1g32` on the 3060 to leave RAM margin. This is still a quality-risk prototype: weight nRMSE is high (~0.58), so it needs opt-in subset tests and graded outputs before any claim.

result_text: cq1g64

Destructive CQ1 prefill smoke on RTX 3060 materialized 1017 entries, reused 789, copied 1806, used 4576.50 MiB compressed CQ1, repacked 12190.50 MiB native bytes, and reported zero materialization/copy/verify/repack failures. It output `????` and was very slow, so broad CQ1 prompt/prefill is a negative. With the conservative default guard, CQ1 entries/copies stayed at zero and the normal launcher was restored.

quality_signal: The phase gate works mechanically and prevents accidental first-token/prompt CQ1. It does not make CQ1 usable yet: warmup 0 is still a quality/perf negative. The next useful test is longer native warmup with CQ1 admitted only after token 50 on prompts where quality can be graded.
setup_text: Commit `/root/ds4` `dc7eaa0 tiering: gate cq1 by prompt hotset`; CQ1 now requires the prompt-derived `observe_promote` hotset unless `DS4_EXPERT_COLD_ALLOW_UNGATED=1`, keeps prompt/prefill native unless `DS4_EXPERT_COLD_RAM_PREFILL=1`, and defaults to `DS4_EXPERT_COLD_NATIVE_TOKENS=50` before cold CQ1 is allowed. Cache fills now receive the real prompt/decode phase instead of being forced permanently prefill-like.
result_text: Build OK: `make ds4-server CUDA_ARCH=sm_86`. RTX 3060 smoke, direct path, cap32, warmup 50: output one token `L`, CQ1 stayed off with `entries=0`, `policy(no_hotset=6,warmup=252)`. Destructive warmup 0 direct path: `entries=252`, `copies=252`, `bytes=1134.00 MiB`, `repacked=1701.00 MiB`, zero failures, one-token output `R`, decode still ~42s. Cache-fill smoke with 1 resident slot and warmup 0 also materialized 252 entries with zero failures and similarly poor speed.
verdict: The phase gate works mechanically and prevents accidental first-token/prompt CQ1. It does not make CQ1 usable yet: warmup 0 is still a quality/perf negative. The next useful test is longer native warmup with CQ1 admitted only after token 50 on prompts where quality can be graded.

quality_signal: Negative policy result: synchronous cold CQ1 materialization/repack on selected/cache misses is the wrong place to pay compression. Keep CQ1 as a payload candidate, but move admission to a breath/exchange actuator with background promotion/demotion.
setup_text: DS4 `/root/ds4` `dc7eaa0`, local RTX 3060, prompt HTML, `DS4_EXPERT_COLD_FORMAT=cq1g32`, `DS4_EXPERT_TIER_POLICY=observe_promote`, `DS4_EXPERT_TIER_PROMOTE_CAP=32`, `DS4_EXPERT_COLD_NATIVE_TOKENS=50`, max 64. Artifact: `runs/ds4/20260709_cq1_parallel/local_3060_cq1_native50`.
result_text: Prompt 64 took 23.335s. First 50 decode tokens ran at 1.52 t/s. After CQ1 became admissible, the last 14 tokens collapsed to 0.06 t/s; total wall 324.028s. CQ1 summary: `entries=1073`, `copies=3612`, `bytes=4828.50 MiB`, `repacked=24381.00 MiB`, failures 0. Output began as HTML but degraded/truncated (`CYBER·AI · NEGO·STO...`).
verdict: Negative policy result: synchronous cold CQ1 materialization/repack on selected/cache misses is the wrong place to pay compression. Keep CQ1 as a payload candidate, but move admission to a breath/exchange actuator with background promotion/demotion.

experiment: Breath exchange observe and one-expert ramp
quality_signal: The exchange signal is exactly the hook needed for dynamic compression, but one-expert-per-token with synchronous mask apply + WRAP is not a default. Next actuator should batch/queue exchange work: promote/decompress experts entering the widened mask and compress experts leaving it, but do this asynchronously and bounded, not by forcing full WRAP every token.
setup_text: DS4 `/root/ds4` `0bdad9a tiering: observe pace breath exchange`; env `DS4_PACE_EXCHANGE_OBSERVE=1` logs old/new mask deltas. Two local smoke runs: (A) `PREFILL_APPLY=1`, `KEEP=23`, `KEEP_STEP=1`, `PREBREATH_EVERY=1`, `WRAP=0`, max 96; (B) `PREFILL_APPLY=0`, same micro-ramp with `WRAP=1`, max 80. Artifacts: `runs/ds4/20260709_cq1_parallel/local_3060_exchange_observe*`.
result_text: A confirmed the bad first-token shape: mask applied at `tok=0`, `K0->K23` demoted 10019 expert slots, then `K23->32` promoted 43 slots per +1K step; quality repeated malformed HTML and total wall was 452.654s. B confirmed proper K0 warmup: `learned tok=50`, `descent tok=51`, then +1K prebreaths. Each +1K promoted about 43 layer-expert slots, but WRAP ran on every micro-step (`6.07 GiB` first touch, then 6.34..8.45 GiB) and the cold first 50 tokens were only 0.11 t/s; total wall 589.391s.
verdict: The exchange signal is exactly the hook needed for dynamic compression, but one-expert-per-token with synchronous mask apply + WRAP is not a default. Next actuator should batch/queue exchange work: promote/decompress experts entering the widened mask and compress experts leaving it, but do this asynchronously and bounded, not by forcing full WRAP every token.

experiment: CPU-lane profiler: cold vs hot mmap, thread scaling, prefetch
result_text: NOT disk-bound: process_io_read_mb=0 for cold AND hot (soft page-cache faults, not disk). Cold/hot 4.05x. Tiny-batch kills parallelism: 8 workers on n=2 experts = 2.97 ms/expert vs 0.85 full batch. 8-worker shared speedup 6.31x (parallel capability present). Inline PrefetchVirtualMemory NOT a fix (3.2ms call, 90% cold time retained) -> only ASYNC ahead-of-time prefetch helps. Root cause of lane slowness = 4x cold-fault x ~3x tiny-batch x no-overlap.
verdict: lane slowness = parallelism + overlap, NOT disk; async miss-reduction is the lever

experiment: H-STATICO: is the ~5 t/s decode ceiling the static/dense fp16 path?
result_text: REFUTED. No supported ~5 t/s hard ceiling: measured steady Q1-hybrid decode reaches 6.96 t/s (G114) and a request-scoped exact-IQ2 run reaches 6.79 t/s (G73). Static path is NOT the wall. BUT those >6 runs are approximate-Q1 or L0/non-promotable; the full/open EXACT anchor is 1.65 t/s (G123). The real blocker is expert transport/serving + quality, not a static ceiling. Cheap settle: 64-tok fixture with CUDA-event/NVTX ranges separating non-MoE static kernels vs routed-MoE on their streams -> causal static_ms vs expert_ms.
verdict: H-STATICO REFUTED — no 5 t/s static wall; the wall is exact-quality above 1.65 (transport+overhead+capacity)

experiment: Compute vs overhead: why keep-8 fits VRAM yet only ~3.5 t/s (owner's skepticism)
result_text: Owner correct: 3.5 t/s all-in-VRAM is NOT a compute limit (active ~10B params ~50 GFLOP/tok = ~1-2% of 3060 peak). Differential decomposition of the 287ms token: 42% CPU host orchestration (121ms, GPU idle) + 49% GPU-blocked (expert H2D copy 76ms/27% EXPOSED-serial + compute 50ms/17% + small-copy 17ms) + 9% launch (26ms). REFUTES both 'static ceiling' and 'pure 70% engine overhead' (real GPU work is ~50%). Expert H2D copy is EXPOSED not hidden (overlap~0). DECISIVE LEVER = VRAM RESIDENCY: fitting the working set removes the exposed per-token copy -> 3090 c1024 = 14-16 t/s, 3080Ti coding-fit = 12 t/s (measured, need >12GB). 3060 is CAPACITY-LOCKED ~3.4 t/s: 12GB can't make the working set resident, pays serial PCIe copy every token. A 2.6x-faster engine -> only +13%; residency -> ~4x. The 25.82 t/s (CLAIM-008) is the fit/prefill transient showing the real potential.
verdict: NOT compute-bound, NOT static, NOT pure-overhead: ~50/50 CPU-orchestration + exposed PCIe copy. 3060 = VRAM-CAPACITY-LOCKED. Reap-loop (keep working set resident + CPU tail, no per-token copy) IS the right lever; also cut the 42% CPU orchestration.

experiment: M0 miss-economics offline sim: owner layout VRAM320+pinned1500 IQ2, live mask W=8 P=8, continuous CPU stager (ORACLE lookahead 8/16/32, 1.5-3 GB/s), 3 long decode replays 9016 tok (40/43 layers) + 3x64tok full-43L audit. Expert=6.75MiB exact (runtime metadata).
result_text: Owner mechanism PARTIALLY validated. (1) Miss 10-20% claim NOT met at owner layout: 27.9% median / 40.0% p90 (74 misses/tok); pinned 2000 -> 22.5%/33.8%; pinned is the lever. (2) CPU-economy claim MET: warm residual = 414 exp/s vs 970 derated = 2.34x headroom @5.6 tok/s design point. (3) KEY: staging does NOT shrink fast-tier miss (29.4->28.7%) but TRANSFORMS it - must-stall (SSD-cold) collapses 2.628 -> 0.050 exp/tok; misses become RAM-warm/CPU-servable. Staging BW needed ~0.1 GB/s actual (trivial vs 1.5). Per-replay stable (25.8-30.8% median). CAVEATS: oracle lookahead (not a predictor), in-sample seed, page-cache optimistic (no finite budget), 40/43 layers scaled, layer-barriers/tiny-batch/join OUT of scope (avg ~1.85 exp/layer < batch-4 threshold - M3 risk). GO/NO-GO from sim: GO only for a CAUSAL predictor-replay prototype on held-out traces + real page-cache budget, NOT yet engine integration.
verdict: PARTIAL-GO: economy holds (CPU covers residual 2.34x, stalls ~0), miss% above claim (27.9 vs 10-20; 22.5 @pinned2000); next = causal predictor replay (M0b), engine work gated on it

experiment: M0b causal predictor replay: heat_ema / mass-pressure(0039) / persistence, tuned ONLY on learn_a (html_cyberpunk), frozen, evaluated HELD-OUT on rust_channel + 3x64tok full-43L audit; finite page-cache LRU (64GiB-12GiB-pinned = ~5.9k/5.5k experts); same gates as M0.
result_text: NO-GO for engine integration. Best causal signal (heat_ema) on held-out rust_channel: miss 50.4% median (vs 27.9% oracle), must-stall 6.019 exp/tok (vs 0.050 oracle, gate <=0.2 FAIL), CPU headroom 1.42x @5.6tok/s (gate >=1.5 FAIL marginal), staging needed p90 0.426 GB/s (pass individually). 3x64 audit aggregate: must-stall 40.7/tok (cold-start dominated), staging p90 2.775 GB/s FAIL. All three causal signals within noise of each other (50.0-50.4%) -> predictor CHOICE is not the issue. BROKEN ASSUMPTION identified: causal decode history cannot pre-warm SSD-cold FIRST/re-entry references - ranking among already-seen experts is fine, cold first-touches are the blocker. NOTE: M0b starts decode with cold cache; the real engine has PREFILL routes before decode t0 (a free causal signal the sim did not use) - next probe M0c tests prefill-seeded staging + prompt-conditioned prior (converges on the owner's SPEX idea).
verdict: NO-GO (honest): oracle economy does NOT survive causality on held-out data; blocker = cold first-references, not predictor ranking; M0c = prefill-seeded staging (the engine's real pre-decode signal) before any engine work

experiment: M0c prefill-seeded staging: replay actual prompt-position routes through finite LRU before decode t0 (page-cache warming + mass-ranked seed of pinned+VRAM), then frozen heat_ema decode. Held-out rust_channel + python_async (audit logs have no prompt routes). No tuning replay at all (fully request-local causal).
result_text: NO-GO on purity gates but decisive decomposition. Owner layout: must-stall 4.7-4.9 exp/tok (gate 0.2 FAIL), CPU headroom 1.37-1.42x (gate 1.5 FAIL marginal), staging p90 0.341 GB/s (PASS). DECOMPOSITION of M0b's 6.02 must-stall: 0.685 (11.4%) prefill-coverable FIRSTs -> prefill seeding kills these to 0.001; 4.047 (67.2%) TRUE SSD-COLD RE-ENTRIES = the dominant blocker (finite RAM: LRU holds ~5.9k of ~11k experts, long-range re-entries fall out); 1.283 prompt-unseen FIRSTs; +0.5/tok CPU overflow appears at p90 bursts. Prefill-window feasibility: seeding 12 GiB needs prefill rate <=5 tok/s at 1.5 GB/s (marginal). Sim chain M0->M0b->M0c CLOSES the understanding phase: mechanism viable but NOT stall-free on this RAM; the 0.2 gate is a PURITY gate - ms translation: ~4.8 stalls x ~2-4ms parallel NVMe = ~10-19ms/tok exposed (~7-10% of a 178ms 5.6tok/s token), NOT a wall. CPU-served ~122 exp/tok = ~13ms wall at 8 workers IF overlapped (M3) with tiny-batch risk at ~3/layer.
verdict: NO-GO on purity gates / VIABLE on ms-economics: residual stalls cost ~10-19ms/tok; blockers ranked = RAM-capacity re-entries > prompt-unseen FIRSTs > CPU burst overflow; design implication = lane-A must be ELASTIC (CPU-compute OR async pinned-H2D per expert, whichever fits under GPU time)

experiment: H-SPEC-A offline: classic speculative decoding feasibility. Prompt-lookup n-gram drafter, acceptance EXACT at temp0 (drafted token accepted iff equals actually-generated token, regex-lexeme proxy tokenizer), on 25 committed code/HTML outputs (10,756 proxy tokens in the 3 named long decodes) + expert-union growth from 10 route sequences / 21,382 decode tokens.
result_text: GO for a tightly-scoped engine spike at n=2 k=2 ONLY (with no-candidate fallback to plain decode). Acceptance n2k2 = 47.8% pooled (0.574 accepted drafts/pass, 1.574 committed/pass); k=8 commits only 1.895/pass while needing 4.32x expert union -> long drafts do NOT pay. Union growth decisively sublinear: 1.63x @k2, 2.66x @k4, 4.32x @k8 (46% below no-overlap ceiling; coherence confirmed). PROJECTIONS (labeled, not measured): cache32 3.4 -> 3.85-4.08 t/s fixed-k (1.13-1.20x), 4.34-4.51 with candidate gating (1.28-1.33x); on M1-residency cost model 4.18-4.65 / 4.68-5.08 gated. Text-dependent even within code (python_async weakest 42.3%). HONESTY: 25.82 anchor used only as optimistic bound (did NOT reproduce on 3060 per keep8_fit_repro REPORT); no prose control in corpus; proxy-tokenizer spread (byte proxy more optimistic) is why verdict is spike-not-ship; degenerate repetitive outputs (95-99% acceptance) excluded from projection basis.
verdict: GO for n2k2 spike (projected +13-33%); NOT the big multiplier (acceptance ~48% caps committed/pass ~1.57); composes with M1; the bigger open question is H-SPEC-B expert-prefetch

experiment: H-SPEC-B speculative expert prefetch (owner's idea: CPU draft as expert-demand oracle for lane B). DEGRADED probe: route CSVs carry NO token ids (pos,layer,e0-5,w0-5 only) and text-position alignment unrecoverable without the exact tokenizer -> token->expert map untestable; fell back to route-context-similarity surrogate. Same M0c finite-LRU harness, held-out rust_channel.
result_text: NO-GO for lane-B redesign, with the decisive structural finding: NO staging signal can reach the 0.2 must-stall purity gate at this layout - even a PERFECT drafter cannot (the linear model needs '1424% acceptance'). The residual ~4.4-4.7 stalls/tok are CAPACITY-STRUCTURAL (LRU 5.9k of 11k experts: re-entries + genuinely novel FIRSTs), not prediction-limited. Surrogate numbers: draft-k16 must-stall 4.381 vs heat_ema 4.696 (-6.7%) vs M0b 6.019; position-similarity ceiling low (top-6 recall 26.1% @h1 -> 16.4% @h16). PREDICTOR INVESTMENT CLOSED: three independent signals (frequency, prefill, speculation-surrogate) converge within 7%; further predictor work is a tangent. The miss answer stays the owner's elastic law (residual stalls ~10-19ms/tok absorbed by CPU workers off-path). CAPTURE GAP noted: add token-id logging to route traces (cheap engine telemetry) to enable the true token->expert test later.
verdict: NO-GO: prediction is SATURATED, residual misses are capacity-structural; elastic-serve (owner's law) is the design answer; spec-dec value lives in H-SPEC-A token acceptance, not prefetch; predictor chapter closed

experiment: H-SPEC-B2 THE REAL token->expert staging test (un-degraded): engine tokenizer (ds4_dump_text_tokenization, tokenizer-only mmap, no inference) on the 3 long texts - alignment PROVEN by exact count match (prompt+completion = route positions: 74+3017, 76+3200, 49+2799). Per-layer token-id->expert map trained on Python+HTML learn_a (6217 tok, 926 ids), held-out rust; prompt-lookup drafted pipeline k=8/16, depth sweep 16/32/64, elastic gates, finite-LRU with pollution.
result_text: NO-GO, and now DEFINITIVE: the bottleneck is not the drafter - THE TOKEN->EXPERT MAP ITSELF IS WEAK. Given the TRUE future token id, top-6 recall is only 15.0% (top-48 51.5%), horizon-invariant; 53% held-out ids unseen in training. Staging replay: best real draft row 4.572 stall/tok vs heat 4.696 = -2.6% (gate >20%); even the PERFECT-token oracle-map row = 4.092 (-12.9%) FAILS the gate - no acceptance level suffices with this map. Small real positives: stage-origin warm hits 0.49->1.84/tok, ~0.25-0.5 ms/tok saved, table only 1.70 MiB / negligible lookup (engineering was viable, physics was not). ROOT CAUSE: MoE routing reads the HIDDEN STATE, not the token identity - the same token routes to different experts by context. Exact expert foreknowledge requires running the model layers ahead (a real draft forward), whose CPU cost exceeds the ~10-19 ms/tok the residual stalls cost. Caveats: map from 2 code replays (transfer risk), prompt-lookup not a neural drafter - but the perfect-token ceiling bounds ALL drafters with this map class.
verdict: NO-GO definitive: token-identity speculation cannot predict context-driven routing (15% top-6 ceiling); miss-chapter CLOSED on real evidence at every branch (M0 oracle/M0b history/M0c prefill/B surrogate/B2 real map); elastic law stands as the answer; tokenizer-alignment infra is a reusable asset

READY-TO-RUN A/B produced: run_hshadow_prefill_ab.ps1 (4-10 min on 3060, same html_cyberpunk request as pod, exact arm vs TRUE 100%-Q1-prefill arm via SELECTED_LOAD without dual-arena/snapshot, ffn_moe_topk all 43 layers at pos0, overlap mean/p10/median early/mid/late; anti-freeze Idle-load patch applied). SHADOW BUDGET (paper): Q1 MoE 87-113 ms/tok + 20-50 attention allowance = 116-176 ms/tok = 5.7-8.6 shadow t/s vs GPU 5 (viable lead) BUT DRAM is the breaker: 885 MB/tok weight stream = 4.4-7.6 GB/s, at/beyond the measured host envelope (6.6-10.8 GB/s) when lane-A is active - full shadow marginal; needs reuse/partial forwards or stays heat-signal.

experiment: H-SHADOW A/B MEASURED on the 3060: Q1-vs-exact ROUTING AGREEMENT, same html_cyberpunk prompt (76 tok) prefill through both arms; Q1 arm source-audited 100% Q1 routed experts (SELECTED_LOAD, dual-arena/snapshot unset); ffn_moe_topk all 43 layers, 3268 position-layers compared; freeze-safe (Idle load).
result_text: Top-6 overlap: mean 47.4% / median 50% / p10 16.7% (2.84 of 6 experts). Layers 0-2 trivially 100% (dense/shared artifact; routed-only mean ~43%). By depth: early(3-13) ~44-64%, mid(14-28) dips to 27-40% (worst L15-17), late(29-42) RECOVERS to 45-53% - no monotone collapse. READING: 3x better than the token-identity map (15%) and ~1.8x the route-context surrogate (26%), but below a compelling threshold (70%) - and this is an UPPER bound for the online shadow (same-token weight-error only; speculative-token divergence would come on top; DRAM budget already marginal at 4.4-7.6 GB/s). DESIGN RESOLUTION: the elastic valve is ITSELF the discovery mechanism - a new expert's first touch is one hidden CPU serve, then reactive heat promotes it into the mask; the shadow's marginal value is saving that single serve (~already absorbed). Full-forward Q1 shadow PARKED (number on record for revisit if long-generation telemetry shows drift outrunning reactive promotion). G73-OPEN rotation = reactive heat + elastic discovery.
verdict: MEASURED 47.4%/50%/16.7 p10 at 100% Q1 prefill share - shadow possible-but-not-compelling; PARKED; reactive elastic+heat rotation wins on marginal cost; last open design question of G73-OPEN resolved

experiment: H-SHADOW token A/B on the 3060: Q1-vs-exact TOP-1 TOKEN agreement, same html_cyberpunk prompt, 64 tokens greedy per arm. Teacher-forcing unavailable in server API -> free-run fallback (k=0). Script analysis step crashed (PS cast bug, cosmetic); agreement computed offline from saved token_ids.
result_text: Q1 CANNOT guess tokens: matched_prefix=0, positionwise 0/64. Exact arm: correct HTML landing page. Q1-100% arm: IMMEDIATE degenerate collapse (': 1.1.1.1...') from the very first token - which, being generated from the TRUE prompt context, is a clean n=1 teacher-forced sample (p_hat first datapoint = 0/1). COHERENT with the 47% routing agreement: Q1 hidden states are real enough to route half-right but per-layer error compounds to the LM head and collapses generation onto degenerate attractors - the same quality wall that retired Q1 from serving, seen from the other side. Irony on record: the free n-gram drafter guesses tokens BETTER than the Q1 model (47.8% vs ~0) but its tokens cannot be routed without a forward. The one-token-ahead shadow mechanism has no cheap closure.
verdict: Q1-as-token-guesser REFUTED (n=1 + immediate degeneration + all prior Q1-quality evidence concordant); shadow PARKED DEFINITIVELY with 3 measured numbers (routing 47%, token ~0, DRAM marginal); G73-OPEN proceeds purely reactive (valve-discovers -> heat-promotes -> rotator-loads)
